summary: Rebased cleanly onto origin/main, moved the adapter-refactor project spec into the canonical discoverable project path, tightened revision-scoped override and delivery contracts, and reordered the migration plan so schema lands before delivery dedupe depends on it.
validation: git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main
ssh -o BatchMode=yes airlock@localhost '... hq spec lock projects/adversarial-review-adapter-refactor/SPEC.md'

addressed.title: Revisionless operator override
addressed.finding: getOverrides returned bare override values without binding them to the subject revision being acted on.
addressed.action: Changed the operator adapter contract to accept expectedRevisionRef, return observedRevisionRef plus actor, eventId, and observedAt, and require the kernel to ignore or fail closed on revision mismatch.

addressed.title: Incomplete delivery key contract
addressed.finding: Public delivery methods did not carry the full revision, round, and kind key needed for safe dedupe and retry.
addressed.action: Introduced DeliveryKey and DeliveryContext and made review, remediation reply, operator notice, retry, and dedupe methods use the same revision-scoped context.

addressed.title: Dedupe migration order inversion
addressed.finding: The comms dedupe carve depended on domain_id, subject_external_id, and revision_ref fields that were scheduled for a later migration.
addressed.action: Inserted an additive ARA-04B schema ticket before ARA-05, made ARA-05 depend on it, and split 30-day replay/new-key preference into the later ARA-07 step.

addressed.title: Unimplementable kernel acceptance rule
addressed.finding: The acceptance criterion banned the raw substring pr, which would also ban unrelated words like prompt and approved.
addressed.action: Replaced the substring ban with an explicit forbidden adapter vocabulary and regex-style concept list while allowing unrelated ordinary words.

addressed.title: Noncanonical dispatch spec path
addressed.finding: The project-shaped spec lived under a noncanonical SPEC-adapter-refactor.md path that project scanners do not discover by default.
addressed.action: Moved the spec, sidecar metadata, plan, and mapping sidecar into projects/adversarial-review-adapter-refactor/ as SPEC.md, SPEC.meta.json, and adjacent plan artifacts; relocked the spec and updated the plan specRef.

reReview.reason: All blocking review findings have direct spec or plan changes and the moved project spec validates through hq.
